title: Leviticus: Feasts and Covenant Faithfulness
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of leviticus: feasts and covenant faithfulness and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study leviticus: feasts and covenant faithfulness and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: How many appointed feasts are listed in Leviticus 23? Answer: seven.
Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The seven-day feast in which Israel lived in booths remembering their wilderness journey; also called Sukkot, it fell at the end of harvest and pointed to God's provision
The fiftieth year commanded in Leviticus 25 when debts were cancelled, slaves freed, and land returned to original owners; a year of liberty reflecting God's ownership of the land
